tool.name: cve_lookup
tool.description:
Search the NVD (NIST National Vulnerability Database) for CVE vulnerabilities. Automatically checks trickest/cve GitHub repo for public PoC exploit links. Returns CWE descriptions for vulnerability classification.
Examples: - cve_lookup(keyword="nginx 1.24") — find CVEs affecting nginx 1.24 - cve_lookup(keyword="wordpress xmlrpc") — find WordPress XML-RPC vulnerabilities - cve_lookup(cve_id="CVE-2024-21298") — look up a specific CVE with PoC links - cve_lookup(keyword="react", cvss_severity="CRITICAL") — find critical React CVEs
Returns for each CVE: - CVE ID, description, CVSS score + severity + vector - CWE weakness IDs with human-readable descriptions - Affected products and versions - References (vendor advisories, patches) - has_public_exploit: whether a public PoC exists - poc_links: GitHub repositories with exploit code (from trickest/cve database) - actively_exploited: whether this CVE is in CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities list - kev: CISA KEV details (vendor, product, date added, ransomware usage)
PRIORITY: CVEs marked actively_exploited=true are confirmed used by real attackers. These should be tested FIRST as they represent the highest real-world risk. When has_public_exploit is true, examine the poc_links to find working exploits.

parameters.parameter: Search keyword — technology name, version, vulnerability type (e.g. 'apache 2.4', 'jwt bypass', 'prototype pollution')
Specific CVE ID to look up (e.g. 'CVE-2024-21298')
Filter by CVSS v3 severity: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, or CRITICAL
